summary: Jongno's outcome mirrored the national mood more than any district-specific factor — consistent with its '정치 1번지' bellwether reputation. Gwak Sang-eon's Roh Moo-hyun family connection provided candidate-level resonance on top of the broader anti-Yoon wave. The seat returned to DPK after Lee Nak-yeon's 2021 mid-term resignation left it briefly open.

issues.label: National mood / mid-term evaluation
issues.description: Jongno's historical role as bellwether means the election-wide Yoon approval issue translates almost directly into local outcome.

issues.label: Candidate profile
issues.description: Gwak Sang-eon's identification as Roh Moo-hyun's son-in-law added progressive-legacy resonance; opponent 최재형 (former Audit Board chief) carried conservative institutional credentials.

issues.label: Media-attention effect
issues.description: National media treat Jongno races as leading indicators, which attracts high-profile candidates and concentrated coverage — may amplify national-mood signal.
